item.title: Short rates have overshot
item.description: Plus the social media lawsuits

item.title: Distressed-debt funds excited about private credit bargains
item.description: Plus, Millennium shifts staff from Dubai, the Trump pain index and Keith Haring in London

item.title: Is THG about to make some mAjOr gAiNs? Over nearly 2,000 words, we struggle to say
item.description: Bulk bye

item.title: G7 crisis talks as Iran war continues to roil global economy
item.description: Also in this newsletter: Drones enter Finnish airspace as Ukraine hits Baltic ports

item.title: FirstFT: Trump says US could ‘take’ Iran oil
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Hungary’s low fraud funds recovery rate and ABN Amro’s chief on bank overhaul

item.title: Private capital: what are the risks?
item.description: As investors seek to retrieve their money, the $22tn industry rejects comparisons with 2008. Regulators aren’t so sure

item.title: UK ministers explore ‘targeted’ energy bill relief for those most in need
item.description: Lower-cost scheme could be delivered through councils under one option being considered

item.title: What to do when the ‘public good’ of information goes bad
item.description: The creation and dissemination of reliable news is at an economic disadvantage

item.title: TotalEnergies made bumper profit on Middle East oil bet
item.description: In March, French group bought every available cargo of crude produced in UAE and Oman for loading in May

item.title: Eli Lilly pushes for NHS drug price increases in return for its UK investment
item.description: US pharmaceutical group ‘optimistic’ about reaching deal with British government

item.title: Ineos flagship project risks delay from Strait of Hormuz crisis
item.description: Disruption comes as investors see Jim Ratcliffe’s chemicals group boosted by energy market ructions from Iran conflict

item.title: WTO reform talks collapse over US-Brazil stand-off
item.description: Dispute over ecommerce tariffs derails agreement on overhaul of global trade body

item.title: Insurers give Emirates ‘outrageously’ cheap war insurance cover
item.description: Dubai-based airline paying additional $100,000 a week while others face far higher charges

item.title: The lords and alleged lawbreakers among MFS’s eclectic borrowers
item.description: Market Financial Solutions’ loan recipients included sportsmen, TV personalities and those accused of financial crimes

item.title: Trapped seafarers suffer as Iran war rages
item.description: Near-closure of Strait of Hormuz has left thousands of ships stuck in the Gulf with limited protections for crew

item.title: Food giants were finally starting to cut prices — then the war began
item.description: Spectre of another surge in inflation leaves struggling consumer goods groups with difficult decisions

item.title: US bets billions of dollars on unproven groups in rare earths deals
item.description: Companies with financial ties to Trump administration figures win major funding deals in push for crucial metals

item.title: The karaoke company, the penny stock investor and the $17bn trucking rout
item.description: Controversial trader John Fife invested in Algorhythm months before its AI announcement triggered a surge in its share price and a market panic

item.title: ABN Amro CEO: ‘You don’t want to be a herbivore in a world of carnivores’
item.description: Marguerite Bérard says Europe must not be left behind as she overhauls Dutch lender with job cuts and AI

item.title: Pro-AI group to spend $100mn on US midterm elections as backlash grows
item.description: November 8 poll set to be battleground over regulation of AI

item.title: Avoid energy protectionism, UK chancellor will tell G7 allies
item.description: Partners urged to ‘act together’ and not ‘weaken collective resilience’ as nations grapple with Strait of Hormuz closure

item.title: Switzerland’s wine-making president pushes to restrict imports
item.description: Effort to boost local producers draws ire from foreign rivals and domestic merchants

item.title: Distressed-debt funds target private credit downturn as ‘greatest opportunity’ since 2008
item.description: Investors anticipate money-making bonanza as sector comes under strain

item.title: Apollo looks beyond New York as it plans second headquarters
item.description: The prominent private capital firm wants to build a new office in a southern US state as it continues to grow

item.title: Eli Lilly to sign $2bn deal for AI drug development with Hong Kong biotech
item.description: Global pharmaceutical companies are aggressively searching for new medicines in China

item.title: The Iran war will cement China’s superpower status
item.description: Beijing’s industrial prowess positions it for economic and diplomatic gains

item.title: Social media has been put on notice
item.description: Legal and legislative pressure is mounting to safeguard young people

item.title: The Pentagon-Anthropic dispute is a test of control
item.description: Should private companies be able to set boundaries around the AI systems we integrate into our lives?

item.title: Ministers explore triggering break clause in Palantir’s NHS contract
item.description: Government is under pressure to eject the controversial American company from NHS England data systems

item.title: In a world of $100 oil, fast fashion loses its defensive charms
item.description: Polyester-heavy retailers could soon start to feel the cost of their exposure to higher oil prices
